comparison.vs12_5: Phase 13 fixes are all working correctly: (1) Referer now shows real show URL instead of bare cap_union_new_show, (2) vm-slide is fetched live from show page HTML, (3) collect lengths vary per attempt indicating randomized fingerprints. However, errorCode 9 persists for ALL successful template matches. The template pool has expanded significantly - at least 5 distinct template families are now in rotation (94, 95, 96, 98 opcodes + parse failures at 162K/199K chars). Template B (94 ops) is most common (~45% of attempts).

newObservations: Nonce is STATIC across all attempts: eda1152f11f1daf0 - this should be per-session and is suspicious
Two very large TDC builds (162790, 199611 chars) cause VM parser to fail with 'Could not identify thisCtx variable'
Unknown 98-opcode templates still generate collect tokens (6456-6552 chars, longer than Template B's ~5200-5300)
Unknown 96-opcode template (SlVCfKSRjkmVXRnTigehmWSaDkeUUNfk) parses but has no template match
Template A (95 ops) appeared only once across all 27 sub-attempts
vm-slide.e201876f.enc.js hash is constant - same VM slide code across all sessions
Collect lengths for Template B: 5216-5344 (varying, confirming randomized hashes)
Collect lengths for unknown 98-op template: 6456-6552 (larger, likely different schema)
diagnosis: errorCode 9 persists despite all Phase 13 fixes working. Top suspects: (1) STATIC NONCE - nonce 'eda1152f11f1daf0' is identical across ALL 27 sub-attempts and all 3 CLI runs, suggesting it is hardcoded or cached rather than derived from each session's sig response. This is the most likely cause of errorCode 9. (2) TLS fingerprinting (JA3/JA4) - Node.js HTTP client has a distinct TLS fingerprint from browsers. (3) Missing or incorrect request headers in the verify POST. (4) Collect token schema mismatch for Template B builds served live vs the cached XTEA params. Investigation priority: FIX NONCE FIRST - verify it comes fresh from each getSig response.
